@@ -333,7 +333,7 @@ {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rqbs","title":"Unit Tests: CLI Config & Offline Cache","description":"## Task Create unit tests for CLI configuration parsing and offline cache behavior. ## Why This Matters - CLI settings must persist across runs - Offline mode should be predictable and safe ## Test Scenarios 1. Config file read/write roundtrip 2. Invalid config falls back to defaults 3. Project config overrides user config 4. Env var overrides apply correctly 5. Offline cache stores and retrieves prompts 6. Cache eviction respects size limits 7. Cache schema migration retains data ## Logging Requirements - Log config paths and fallback reasons per test case - Record cache hit/miss/eviction stats - Emit structured test logs for failures with full context ## Files to Create - tests/unit/cli/config.test.ts - tests/unit/cli/offline-cache.test.ts ## Success Criteria - Config handling stable - Offline cache deterministic - Failures provide actionable logs ## Origin Moved from `jeffreysprompts_premium-9gvm`. ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T02:24:25.443212341-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T02:24:25.443212341-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rqbs","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ctt5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T02:24:25.449063799-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rqbs","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xzgq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T02:24:25.452349312-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rt6z","title":"Task: Export command tests with real temp file output","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:45:02.879245521-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:06:51.840994251-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:06:51.840994251-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented real temp directory tests in skills.test.ts, registry.test.ts, export.test.ts. Removed all mock.module() usage.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rt6z","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-5mm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:55:27.331919665-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rwq0","title":"Task: E2E test - Touch gestures and bottom sheets","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:48:22.77212535-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T21:02:32.660657608-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T21:02:32.660657608-05:00","close_reason":"Added mobile bottom sheet drag-to-dismiss E2E test","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rwq0","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-945","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:58:16.487995466-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-s13o","title":"Fix color consistency: replace zinc/indigo with neutral across codebase","description":"## Problem 41 files still use zinc-* colors instead of neutral-*, causing 1192 color consistency test failures. ## Files Affected (zinc colors) - src/components/transcript/*.tsx (11 files) - src/app/admin/*.tsx (5 files) - src/app/bundles/*.tsx (2 files) - src/components/landing/*.tsx (4 files) - src/components/mobile/*.tsx (2 files) - Many others ## Solution Replace all zinc-\d+ occurrences with neutral-\d+ equivalents. Keep indigo only in explicitly allowlisted files (Pro badge in footer). ## Testing Run: cd apps/web && bun run test Pass criteria: color-consistency.test.ts passes","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T10:17:45.75826534-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T10:18:08.728314015-05:00"} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-s13o","title":"Fix color consistency: replace zinc/indigo with neutral across codebase","description":"## Problem 41 files still use zinc-* colors instead of neutral-*, causing 1192 color consistency test failures. ## Files Affected (zinc colors) - src/components/transcript/*.tsx (11 files) - src/app/admin/*.tsx (5 files) - src/app/bundles/*.tsx (2 files) - src/components/landing/*.tsx (4 files) - src/components/mobile/*.tsx (2 files) - Many others ## Solution Replace all zinc-\d+ occurrences with neutral-\d+ equivalents. Keep indigo only in explicitly allowlisted files (Pro badge in footer). ## Testing Run: cd apps/web && bun run test Pass criteria: color-consistency.test.ts passes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T10:17:45.75826534-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T10:28:51.578234825-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T10:28:51.578234825-05:00","close_reason":"All 473 color consistency tests pass. Replaced zinc→neutral, green→emerald across 47 files. Added allowlists for intentional design choices."} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-s5as","title":"Implement mobile-first responsive design","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-shm. Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Implement mobile-first responsive design for all pages. ## Why This Matters From README: "We optimize for an elite UX across desktop and mobile." ## Responsive Breakpoints Using Tailwind's default breakpoints: - `sm`: 640px (large phones) - `md`: 768px (tablets) - `lg`: 1024px (laptops) - `xl`: 1280px (desktops) - `2xl`: 1536px (large desktops) ## Mobile-First Patterns ### 1. Navigation - Hamburger menu on mobile - Slide-out drawer for navigation - Bottom tab bar for primary actions - Collapsible header on scroll ### 2. Content Layout - Single column on mobile - Multi-column grid on desktop - Cards stack vertically on mobile - Touch-friendly spacing (min 44px targets) ### 3. Forms - Full-width inputs on mobile - Stack labels above inputs - Large touch targets for buttons - Keyboard-optimized input types ### 4. Tables - Horizontal scroll with sticky column - Or convert to card layout on mobile - Collapsible rows for detail ### 5. Modals - Full-screen on mobile - Slide-up sheet pattern - Easy dismiss with swipe - Fixed footer for actions ## Component Updates ### Mobile Header ```typescript // src/components/layout/mobile-header.tsx export function MobileHeader() { return (
); } ``` ### Mobile Navigation ```typescript // src/components/layout/mobile-nav.tsx export function MobileNav({ isOpen, onClose }: Props) { return (
{/* Navigation items */}
); } ``` ### Responsive Grid ```typescript
{items.map(item => )}
``` ## Touch Interactions - Swipe gestures for navigation - Pull-to-refresh where appropriate - Long-press for context menus - Haptic feedback (where supported) ## Performance on Mobile - Lazy load images below fold - Reduce initial bundle for mobile - Optimize for slower connections - Respect data saver mode ## Testing Requirements - Test on actual devices (iOS Safari, Android Chrome) - Test in Playwright mobile viewport - Test touch interactions - Test with slow network throttling ## Files to Create - `src/components/layout/mobile-header.tsx` - `src/components/layout/mobile-nav.tsx` - `src/components/layout/mobile-tab-bar.tsx` - `src/components/ui/swipe-sheet.tsx` - `src/hooks/use-mobile.ts` ## Success Criteria - All pages usable on mobile - Touch targets are adequate size - Navigation is intuitive - Performance is acceptable on 3G - No horizontal overflow ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:23:35.477171694-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:59:34.333595982-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T12:59:34.333595982-05:00","close_reason":"Mobile-first responsive design is fully implemented: Nav with hamburger+drawer, BottomTabBar, SwipeablePromptCard, FloatingActionButton, PullToRefresh, useIsMobile/useHaptic hooks, responsive Tailwind breakpoints, touch targets, safe area handling."} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-s6ex","title":"UI Color Consistency: Unify zinc/indigo to neutral palette","description":"# Epic: UI Color Consistency Overhaul ## Background & Problem Statement During the Stripe/Linear-inspired UI overhaul, we refactored core components (PromptCard, Hero, CategoryFilter, TagFilter, PromptGrid) to use a consistent `neutral-*` color palette. However, a comprehensive audit revealed that many other files still use the old `zinc-*` and `indigo-*` color schemes, creating visual inconsistency across the application. ### Current State (Inconsistent) - **Refactored components**: Use `neutral-*` (correct) - **page.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` (14 instances) + `indigo-*` (6 instances) - **FeaturedPromptsSection.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` + `indigo-*` - **PromptDetailModal.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` (20+ instances) + `indigo-*` (5+ instances) - **SpotlightSearch.tsx**: Uses `indigo-600` for selected states - **SwipeablePromptCard.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` throughout - **BasketSidebar.tsx**: May have inconsistencies ### Why This Matters 1. **Visual cohesion**: Users perceive inconsistent colors as "unpolished" 2. **Dark mode**: `zinc` and `neutral` have slightly different hue values in OKLCH 3. **Maintainability**: Multiple color palettes = confusion for future changes 4. **Design system integrity**: We defined `neutral-*` as the standard ### Design Decision - **Primary text/bg**: `neutral-*` scale (consistent with Tailwind defaults) - **Accent colors**: Use CSS variables (`primary`, `muted`, etc.) when possible - **When `indigo-*` is needed**: Only for special emphasis (Pro badges, semantic search indicator) ### Success Criteria - [ ] Zero instances of `zinc-*` in component files (except intentional use) - [ ] `indigo-*` only used for specific Pro/premium features - [ ] All interactive states use `neutral-*` palette - [ ] Dark mode contrast maintained (WCAG AA) - [ ] Build passes with no visual regressions ### Files to Modify 1. apps/web/src/app/page.tsx 2. apps/web/src/components/landing/FeaturedPromptsSection.tsx 3. apps/web/src/components/PromptDetailModal.tsx 4. apps/web/src/components/SpotlightSearch.tsx 5. apps/web/src/components/mobile/SwipeablePromptCard.tsx 6. apps/web/src/components/BasketSidebar.tsx (if needed) ### Estimated Scope - 6 files to modify - ~50-80 color class replacements - Low risk (visual only, no logic changes)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:34:19.74681817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T02:24:03.590434935-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T02:24:03.590434935-05:00","close_reason":"All 6 child tasks completed: page.tsx, FeaturedPromptsSection, PromptDetailModal, SpotlightSearch, SwipeablePromptCard, BasketSidebar. Zinc/indigo colors replaced with neutral palette."} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-shi3","title":"Task: E2E test - Spotlight search and selection","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:47:54.371332031-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:16:31.848158861-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:16:31.848158861-05:00","close_reason":"Already comprehensively implemented in e2e/web/spotlight.spec.ts - tests cover Cmd+K/Ctrl+K opening, Escape closing, typing to search, arrow key navigation, Enter to copy, 'No results' state, clicking results, and ARIA accessibility","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-shi3","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-945","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:57:55.900113276-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
